hypothesis: If real-time commodity price feeds and geopolitical supply-chain disruption indices were integrated directly into the analysis module as dynamic input variables, then the workflow would predict reactive switches in optimal reagent selection (e.g., transitioning from CaO/SiO2 to Ca3SiO5/Si3N4) at quantifiable price-ratio thresholds before such disruptions occur, and these predicted switching thresholds will differ significantly from those derived using static average market prices, thereby demonstrating that temporal price volatility—not mean price alone—is the dominant determinant of long-term economic resilience in solid-state synthesis route selection.
